title: CI scaffold: SHACL + Biolink-conformance + provenance-completeness + not-advice-label lint
context: Automated gates enforce the project's hard constraints (provenance completeness, allow-list compliance, not-advice labeling) on every change. Only open / public-domain / openly-licensed sources (CIViC CC0, Open Targets CC0, Reactome, license-checked PMC open-access subset); COSMIC, OncoKB, DrugBank, any non-open DB and all controlled-access / patient-level data (dbGaP, EGA, biobanks, EHR) are out of scope and refused. No patient data; all therapeutic-target content is research evidence labeled 'not medical advice'. See PLAN.md.
objective: Build the CI scaffold that validates exported data for SHACL conformance, Biolink conformance, provenance completeness, allow-list compliance, and the not-advice label.
acceptanceCriteria: CI fails on any assertion lacking a provenance link or source-release stamp.
CI runs SHACL validation + Biolink-conformance on exported edges.
CI rejects data referencing a source not marked approved.
CI fails on any therapeutic-target assertion missing the 'not medical advice' label + evidence level.
output: CI workflow + validation scripts (SHACL, Biolink, provenance, allow-list, not-advice lint), committed via PR.
